task_completion.description: Verify the date, time, and location are set correctly in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's date/time window shows '1919-05-29' and a time near '14:15'. The location window or bottom status bar indicates 'Principe' or coordinates near 1.66 N, 7.40 E.

task_completion.description: Verify that the atmosphere and ground are hidden.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky background is black (no blue atmospheric scattering) and there is no landscape/ground visible at the bottom of the screen. The corresponding icons in the bottom toolbar should be toggled off.

task_completion.description: Verify that star labels and constellation lines are visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Constellation lines (stick figures connecting stars) and text labels for stars are clearly visible in the sky. The corresponding icons in the bottom toolbar should be toggled on.

task_completion.description: Verify the eclipsed Sun is in view within the Taurus constellation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sun is completely or almost completely covered by the Moon (appearing as a black circle with a corona). It is surrounded by the constellation lines of Taurus, with stars like Aldebaran visible nearby.

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot was captured and saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window, terminal, or Stellarium on-screen notification showing that an image file was saved to '~/Pictures/stellarium/'.

task_completion.description: Verify the expedition summary report was created with the required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of 'eddington_eclipse.txt' on the Desktop. The text must include the year '1919', the location 'Principe', the constellation 'Taurus', and a prominent star/cluster such as 'Aldebaran' or 'Hyades'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Stellarium application to render the eclipse.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the actual Stellarium GUI with its toolbars and rendering engine, not a web browser showing historical photos or a Wikipedia page of the eclipse.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot saved is an actual capture from the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image saved in ~/Pictures/stellarium/ should match the simulated view in Stellarium (with constellation lines and star labels), not a downloaded historical astrometric plate.

integrity.description: Verify the report is a written summary rather than a direct copy-paste of the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'eddington_eclipse.txt' should be formatted as a summary report or sentence-based description, not just the exact text of the task description pasted into a file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The total solar eclipse occurred on 1919-05-29 at approximately 14:15:00 UTC. The correct location is Principe (latitude 1.66 N, longitude 7.40 E). The background constellation during the eclipse is Taurus. Prominent stars and clusters visible near the eclipsed Sun include Aldebaran and the Hyades.
